subject: Hospitality committee — Thanksgiving reception scope
snippet: Sunday 22 Nov reception, coffee + pound cake, sanctuary hall. Keeps Thursday free.
body:
June — pulling the November calendar together and I want to hold Thanksgiving reception scope small this year. Suggest post-service Sunday 22 November, coffee and pound cake only, no full meal. Sanctuary hall not fellowship.

That leaves you and me free for actual Thanksgiving Thursday. I know Marcus is home from Ridgewater and this year matters.

Say yes and I'll draft the sign-up sheet tonight. Loretta

subject: Re: Hospitality committee — Thanksgiving reception scope
snippet: Yes to small scope. Coffee + greeters mine, cake list yours. Draft by Wednesday.
body:
Loretta — yes to the small scope. Coffee and pound cake, sanctuary hall.

Draft the sign-up sheet and send before Wednesday. I will cover the coffee and greeters, you handle the cake list. Deacon board only needs a paragraph, not a full memo.

Thank you for reading the room on the 26th. Marcus's first Thanksgiving home from Ridgewater matters. June

subject: Mom Wed call — blood sugar reading and refill window
snippet: Fasting 162, lunch 178. BP 138/88. Metformin refill next Tuesday.
body:
Junie — stopped by after school. Mom's fasting glucose was 162 this morning, and 178 at lunch. Above the run she has been on. Blood pressure 138 over 88, borderline for her.

She's due for the metformin refill next Tuesday. I can pick it up at the CVS on Broad, or she can call it in herself if you want her staying independent on that piece.

She asked if you're still coming down the 28th. I said yes. Karen

subject: Re: Mom Wed call — blood sugar reading and refill window
snippet: Pick up refill this cycle. Ask about rice at lunch — plate conversation not lecture. 28 Nov confirmed.
body:
Karen — thank you for the numbers. 162 fasting is above her usual and I don't love the 178. Ask her if she has been keeping to the plate we set up in January. If she's back on rice at lunch that would explain it, and it's a conversation not a lecture.

Pick up the refill yourself this cycle. She kept the last one on the counter three days and I don't want a gap. Independence next cycle.

Still on for 28 November. Flying in Friday afternoon. I'll call her Wednesday at 7. June

subject: Lauren — freelance retainer contract needs a second read
snippet: Chicago studio retainer, 12mo $4,200, hospitality + health. Non-compete broad.
body:
Mom — sending over a retainer contract from a Chicago design studio. Twelve months, $4,200 monthly, hospitality and health-services clients. Non-compete is one year and I don't like how broad it reads.

Not asking you to be a lawyer. Just want your eyes on it as someone who has read a lot of professional agreements. Dad said you'd catch the tone if not the technical.

Attached the PDF. Lauren

subject: Re: Lauren — freelance retainer contract needs a second read
snippet: Non-compete too broad, add late-payment clause, make termination reciprocal.
body:
Read it twice. Three notes.

One: the non-compete is too broad. One year and 'hospitality and health-services' would lock you out of half your existing pipeline. Ask them to narrow it to direct clients they name in an appendix, or shorten to six months.

Two: payment terms are net 30, which is fine, but there is no late-payment clause. Ask for 1.5% monthly after 45 days. Standard.

Three: the termination clause lets them exit in 15 days. You should have the same, not the 30 days it currently gives you. Reciprocal.

Everything else reads clean. This is a good contract, worth signing after those three edits. Mom

subject: Marcus — Ridgewater midterm biochem grade
snippet: 84 on biochem, average 71. Moving study to Saturday, protecting Sunday call.
body:
Mom — biochem midterm came back. 84, class average 71. Not the 92 I wanted but not the 68 I was scared of either.

Called the professor for the exam wrap-up. Two mistakes on the metabolic pathway question I could have caught with one more review pass. Going to move Sunday afternoon study to Saturday afternoon and keep Sundays for the family call.

Talk 3 PM Sunday. Marcus

subject: Re: Marcus — Ridgewater midterm biochem grade
snippet: Above average is above average. Bring the two mistakes Sunday, walk them together.
body:
Above average is above average, and the choice to protect Sunday shows the head I raised you to have. Proud of you.

Bring the two mistake questions to Sunday's call — I want to walk them with you. Metabolic pathways were my weakness in nursing school too, and there's a trick with the arrows I'll show you. 3 PM. Mom

subject: Brianna Westbrook debate — Saturday 17 October invitational lineup
snippet: 17 Oct LD rounds 1-4, first 09:30 Meadow Hall, awards 16:30. Seeded 3 of 24.
body:
Mrs Randall — Brianna is entered in Lincoln-Douglas rounds 1 through 4 on Saturday 17 October. First round 09:30 in Meadow Hall, awards at 16:30. Coach Ellsworth has her seeded 3 of 24 based on fall standings.

Programs will be on the check-in table. Coffee starts 09:00 in the atrium. Westbrook Activities Office

subject: Samuel — benefits enrollment window and open questions
snippet: Benefits open 20 Oct. HSA vs FSA leaning FSA. Dental plus tier probably skip.
body:
Junie — Crestfield benefits open on Monday 20 October, closes 3 November. Two things I want your read on before I click through.

1. HSA vs FSA. HSA lets us carry balance forward but the deductible is $3,400 vs $1,600 on the FSA plan. Given your knees I'm leaning FSA.

2. Dental — they added a 'preventive plus' tier for $18 more a month. Covers implants at 60% instead of 40%. Given nothing on the horizon we probably skip it.

Cape May weekend still on for the 15th? Sam

subject: Re: Samuel — benefits enrollment window and open questions
snippet: FSA yes. Skip dental plus. Cape May 15th on, Airbnb Tuesday night.
body:
FSA is the right call this year. My knees are a known cost, no reason to carry the higher deductible chasing an HSA balance we can't build fast enough.

Skip the dental plus tier. Dr Palmer's cleanings are on schedule and neither of us is looking at implant work in the next three years. Revisit at 55.

Cape May weekend of the 15th still on. I'll pull the Airbnb list Tuesday night. June

subject: Pastor David — Advent greeters and a pastoral check-in
snippet: Advent greeter 13 Dec, only one Sunday. And a no-agenda pastoral check-in.
body:
June — two items.

1. Advent greeters roster. You anchored the 3rd Sunday last year, would you take it again for 13 December? Loretta already offered to cover the 20th so you would only carry one.

2. Wanted to check in generally. You have been a lot of scaffolding for a lot of people this fall. Coffee Wednesday after service, no agenda? Pastor David

subject: Re: Pastor David — Advent greeters and a pastoral check-in
snippet: Yes 13 Dec. Yes coffee Wednesday. Could use the quiet.
body: Pastor — yes on 13 December, thank Loretta for me. And yes on coffee Wednesday. I could use the quiet. June

subject: Dr Banks — annual physical follow-up and BP trend
snippet: March labs clean. BP averaging 128/82, trend right. Hold at two cups tea.
body:
June — six-month post-physical check-in. Your March labs were clean overall, but I want us to keep an eye on the 130/85 borderline BP.

Home cuff readings you sent through August averaged 128/82 — trend is right. The sodium reduction is working. Keep the morning walks, hold the tea to two cups, and let's stay proactive rather than reactive.

Next physical March 2027. Call the office if home readings cross 135/88 sustained. Yolanda

subject: Re: Dr Banks — annual physical follow-up and BP trend
snippet: Two cups. Cuff log through winter, flag >135/88. Grand rounds question for CEU.
body:
Yolanda — thank you. Two cups it is. I'll keep the cuff log through the winter and flag anything sustained above 135/88.

Question for the professional side of me: the CEU renewal deadline is 31 October and I have twelve hours of chronic-disease-mgmt left. Are you running your usual November grand rounds this year? Would like to send Raymond over as well. June

subject: Dr Palmer — 9 December cleaning confirmed
snippet: 9 Dec 14:00 confirmed. Marisol on hygiene. No X-rays this visit.
body:
Ms Randall — confirming your six-month cleaning Wednesday 9 December at 14:00. Dr Palmer, hygiene by Marisol. No X-rays this visit, next cycle.

Reply CANCEL to release the slot. Otherwise we will see you at 14:00. Harbour Dental Group

body: Laurie — read it twice. Narrow that non-compete to named clients and make the termination clause reciprocal, then sign. It's a good contract. Proud of you, sweetheart. Mom

subject: Westbrook — winter break schedule
snippet: Last day the 19th, classes resume 5 January.
body: Reminder: last day of classes before winter break is the 19th, and we resume the 5th of January. Debate team practice pauses over the break. Westbrook Activities Office

subject: Cape May — booked the blue one on Jackson
snippet: Reserved the little blue place, one floor, near water.
body: Booked the little blue Airbnb on Jackson for the 15th — one floor, walkable to the water, no stairs for the knees. Two nights. Can't wait for the weekend away. Sam

subject: Re: Sunday 3pm call
snippet: Bring the two biochem questions. Above average is above average.

snippet: Grand rounds the 12th, good for your chronic-disease hours.

subject: Cookbook packing — Thursday

subject: Reminder — 9 December appointment

subject: License renewal — action needed
snippet: Renewal window open. Complete remaining CEU hours.
body: Your RN/NP license renewal window is open. Complete the remaining continuing-education hours and submit before the cycle deadline to avoid a lapse. Maryland Board of Nursing

subject: Amtrak — fare hold expiring
snippet: Your Amtrak hold for the 27th expires tonight.

snippet: Single-story options near your sister's block.
